California
Fair P01itical
Practices Comrnission
November 199 0
charles G- Abdelnour, J. D.
city clerk, city of San Diego
City edministration Bui Iding
202 C Street
San Diego, CA 921,oL
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-90-67 0
Dear Mr- Abdelnour:
You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Politica} Reforn Act.1
OUESTION
Is Together for Linda Bernhardt, a controll-ed committee
formed to support Linda Bernhardt's reel-ection to office, required
to file a campaign statenent under Section 84200.5(f) if it
receives contributions or nakes expenditures in opposition to an
ef f ort to recall, I'Is. Bernhardt?
CONCLUS TON
only committees formed primarily to support or oppose the
qualification of a ballot tneasure, including a recall measure, are
required to file under Section 8420O.5(f).
FACTS
The Together for Linda Bernhardt committee filed a statenent
of organization on August 15, 1990, with the Secretary of State,s
office. 
According to the statement of organization, the cornmittee
was formed to support Linda Bernhardt, s reelection to the city
counci I .
An effort was made to place the question of whether Ms.
Bernhardt should be recaLled on the bal-l-ot. The Together for
Linda Bernhardt used its funds in opposition to the qualification
of the recall- measure .
1 
Go.r"..rment Code Sections 81OOO-91015. AII statutory
-references are to the covernment code unLess otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. AII references to regulations ar6 to title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
42S I Strcct,Stlitc 800 ● P.o.Box807 0 S.lcr.In、cntO CA 95804‐0807 0
(tl1 6)322 ´5660


Charles G. Abdelnour, J. D.
Page Two
ANALYSIS
Section 842oo.5(f) requires committees formed prirnarily to
support or oppose the qualification of a ballot measure to file 
a
carnpaign statement no later than 21 days after any petitions are
tifla 
in connection with the attenpt to place the measure on the
balIot, or 21, days after the deadline for filing those petitions,
whichever is earlier.
A recall measure is included in the Act's definition of
trneasure. tt Section 82043 def ines a rrmeasurert as:
...any constitutional amendment or other proposition which is
subrnilted to a popul-ar vote at an election by action of a
Iegislative body, or which is subnitted or is intended to be
su6mitted to a poputar vote at an election by initiative,
referendum or rlcltl 
procedure whether or not it qualifies
for the ballot.
Since the Together for Linda Bernhardt committee is not a
conmittee prirnarily formed to support or opPose the qualification
of a ballot measure, it is not required to f il-e a carnpaign
statement under Section 84200.5(f). Ho\./ever, al1 pa)rnents the
committee makes, including those to oppose the qualification of
the recall measure, must be reported on the committee's regular
semi-annual or pre-election campaign staternents. (Section 84211.)
The Political 
Reform Act does not
from using campaign funds to oppose an
fron office. 
(Honq Advice Letter, No.
If you have additional questions,
322-5662.
prohibit an off iceholder
effort to recalL him or her
